text.*:
Awards and honors

[edit]
Main article: List of career achievements by LeBron James
NBA[1]
  • 4× NBA champion: 2012, 2013, 2016, 2020
  • 4× NBA Finals Most Valuable Player: 2012, 2013, 2016, 2020
  • 4× NBA Most Valuable Player: 2009, 2010, 2012, 2013
  • 22× NBA All-Star: 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026
  • 3× NBA All-Star Game MVP: 2006, 2008, 2018
  • 21× All-NBA selection:
    • 13× First team: 2006, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2020
    • 4× Second team: 2005, 2007, 2021, 2025
    • 4× Third team: 2019, 2022, 2023, 2024
  • 6× NBA All-Defensive selection:
    • 5× First Team: 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013
    • Second Team: 2014
  • NBA Rookie of the Year: 2004
  • NBA All-Rookie First Team: 2004
  • NBA scoring leader: 2008
  • NBA assists leader: 2020
  • 3× NBA minutes leader: 2007, 2017, 2018
  • J. Walter Kennedy Citizenship Award: 2017
  • NBA 75th Anniversary Team
  • NBA Cup winner: 2023
  • NBA Cup Most Valuable Player: 2023
  • 41× NBA Player of the Month[2]
  • 69× NBA Player of the Week[3]
USA Basketball[4]
  • 3× Olympic Gold Medal winner: 2008, 2012, 2024
  • 2004 Olympic Bronze Medal winner
  • FIBA Men's Olympics Most Valuable Player: 2024
  • FIBA Men's Olympics All-Star Five: 2024
  • 2006 FIBA World Championship Bronze Medal winner
  • 2007 FIBA Americas Championship Gold Medal winner
  • 2012 USA Basketball Male Athlete of the Year
  • Commemorative banner in Miami's American Airlines Arena (for his 2012 gold medal won as a member of the Miami Heat)
High school[5]
  • 2003 National Champion
  • 3× OHSAA Champion: 2000, 2001, 2003
  • 2003 Naismith Prep Player of the Year[6]
  • 2× Mr. Basketball USA: 2002, 2003
  • 2× Gatorade National Player of the Year 2002, 2003
  • 2× USA Today High School Player of the Year 2002, 2003
  • 3× Ohio Mr. Basketball: 2001, 2002, 2003
  • 3× USA Today All-USA First Team: 2001, 2002, 2003
  • 2× PARADE High School Player of the Year: 2002, 2003
  • 2003 McDonald's National Player of the Year[7]
  • 2003 McDonald's High School All-American[8]
  • 2003 McDonald's All-American Game MVP
  • 2003 EA Sports Roundball Classic MVP[9]
  • 2003 Jordan Brand Classic MVP[9]
  • No. 23 retired by St. Vincent–St. Mary[10]
  • St. Vincent–St. Mary home basketball court named The LeBron James Arena[11]
  • 2001 OHSAA Division IV All-Ohio First Team Offense (football)[12]
Media
  • AP Athlete of the Decade (2010s)
  • 4× AP Athlete of the Year (2013, 2016, 2018, 2020)[13]
  • 3× Sports Illustrated Sportsperson of the Year (2012, 2016, 2020)[14][15]
  • 2012 Sporting News Athlete of the Year[16]
  • 3× Sporting News NBA MVP (2006, 2009, 2010)[17][18]
  • 2004 Sporting News Rookie of the Year[19]
  • Sports Illustrated NBA All-Decade First Team (2000s)[20]
  • 2× Hickok Belt winner: 2012, 2013
  • 20× ESPY Award winner in various categories (16 individually, four as part of a team)[21]
  • 9× BET Sportsman of the Year Award winner
  • 2020 Time Athlete of the Year
  • Co-flag bearer for the United States at the 2024 Summer Olympics' opening ceremony with Coco Gauff
NAACP Image Awards
  • 2017 Jackie Robinson Award
  • 2021 President's Award
Sports Emmy Awards
  • 2020 Outstanding Long Sports Documentary (as executive producer of What's My Name? – Muhammed Ali Part 1)[22]
  • 2021 Outstanding Edited Sports Series (as executive producer of The Shop: Uninterrupted)[23]
  • 2023 Outstanding Long Documentary (as executive producer of The Redeem Team)[24]
Halls of Fame
  • Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame – Class of 2025 as a member of the 2008 United States men's Olympic basketball team ("Redeem Team")[25]
  • St. Vincent–St. Mary Hall of Fame – Class of 2011[26]
Fashion
  • First black man to appear on the cover of Vogue, posing with supermodel Gisele Bündchen on the April 2008 edition[27]
  • Honorary chair of the 2025 Met Gala in Manhattan[28]
State/Local
  • 6× Cleveland Sports Awards Professional Athlete of the Year: 2004, 2005, 2006, 2009, 2015, 2016[29]
  • South Main Street in downtown Akron renamed King James Way[30]
  • Six-story commemorative banner in downtown Akron[30]
  • Featured on Space Jam inspired mural in Akron near his alma mater (St. Vincent St. Mary) and his I Promise school[31]
  • Featured on "Cleveland is the Reason" mural in downtown Cleveland (with other notable Cleveland area figures)[32]
  • Honorary lockers at Ohio State's football and basketball facilities[33][34]
  • LeBron James Home Court Museum in Akron, Ohio[35]
